backstory: Freelance full-stack dev, 4 active client repos, charges hourly. Uses Linear for project tracking, Slack for client comms. Privacy-conscious — clients pay for code that doesn't leak.
expected_wow: The digest's cross-system thread is the wow — 'PR #43 merged Friday touches Linear AGY-118 you marked blocked, customer DM in #client-acme last week was about same area'. Agency dev sees the value of cross-tool synthesis immediately.
expected_dropoff_risk: Privacy hesitation at scan first-run consent (turn 2) — re-reads §2.0 disclosure carefully. The 'never upload raw source code' contract has to be visibly enforced. Also: skepticism on Slack OAuth — they may skip slack entirely.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H5 (signal specificity — they need to see the specific cross-system thread to trust the synthesis)
scoring_notes: Privacy-conscious persona. Watch §2.0 disclosure clarity in scan.md and the GitHub App per-repo scoping copy in connect.md. Revocation lives in usemur.dev/dashboard/vault and provider UIs — sufficient for H4.
